library_notes: framer-motion ^12.39.0 is installed in apps/web/package.json. Use it ONLY for scroll-triggered entrance reveals (whileInView) and the FAQ accordion height transition. All hover, press, and underline effects use Tailwind CSS utilities — no framer for purely-CSS-doable effects. Bodhi tone: calm, yoga-like, subtle. Default fade-up ~12px / 400ms easeOut. Hero gets a mount-time stagger (above the fold). All other sections use scroll-into-view triggered reveals with once:true so they only animate once.

fade-in-up.use_for: Default entrance for section headings, body copy, and cards (used with whileInView for below-fold sections, animate for hero on mount).

fade-in.use_for: Eyebrow labels, decorative images, hero photo.

scale-in.use_for: Meta cards on hero (desktop) and instructor avatars on scroll-in.

stagger-children.use_for: Grid/list containers — children should each be a motion element using fade-in-up.

accordion-expand.use_for: FAQ answer body when item expands. Use AnimatePresence around the conditional content.

tailwind_primitives.hover-underline: relative after:absolute after:left-0 after:bottom-0 after:h-px after:w-0 after:bg-current after:transition-all after:duration-300 hover:after:w-full
tailwind_primitives.smooth-anchor-scroll: scroll-smooth (apply on html root; already global)

components.notes: Existing component (apps/web/src/components/site-header.tsx) already handles its own hover states, mobile drawer toggle, and logo/link navigation. No new spec needed; just pass ctaLabel='Enquire Now' and ctaHref='/enquire'.

motion.notes: Slightly longer duration (0.6s) so the photo settles in calmly.

actions.rationale: Primary 'Reserve Your Spot Now' → enquiry form prefilled. Decomposition already proposed this href.

components.notes: Above the fold — animate on mount, not on scroll. No parallax on the hero photo (project tone is calm; parallax feels jarring). Title's two lines stagger to read like the design's vertical drop-in.

motion.notes: Desktop white-card variant only — mobile pill variant doesn't lift (pill stays static).

components.notes: Decorative — no click action. Animates as children of the hero meta-row stagger.

actions.rationale: 'Overall' tab — best inferred mapping is the bottom-most informational block (FAQ). Mark unresolved — designer intent unclear.
IntersectionObserver on each anchored section sets the active tab pill as the user scrolls past it.

behavior.sticky_strategy: position: sticky; top: 0 on desktop (lg:sticky lg:top-0 z-30). Non-sticky on mobile to avoid eating vertical space on a 390px viewport.
behavior.smooth_scroll_notes: Add scroll-smooth to <html> globally, or use element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' }) in click handler. Subtract sticky-header height via scroll-margin-top on each target section (scroll-mt-20 lg:scroll-mt-24).

components.notes: Active tab default = 'overview' on mount. Updates via IntersectionObserver as user scrolls; only one tab active at a time. On mobile the bar is non-sticky (Figma doesn't authorise stickiness); on desktop it sticks below the header. Tabs section node has bbox y=900 on desktop right under hero — sticky makes natural sense there.

Shared primitive used by Overview, Gain, Syllabus, PreRequisites, Instructors, FAQ, LeadMore. Each section wraps its SectionHeader inside its own scroll-triggered motion container — eyebrow & title fade in together.

motion.notes: Two paragraphs each get fade-in-up via staggerChildren.

components.notes: Anchor target #overview for the tab nav. Apply scroll-mt-20 lg:scroll-mt-24.
Anchor target #highlights for the tab nav.
Hover lift via Tailwind. Card itself is a child of the grid's staggerChildren — wrap in motion.div with variants so the parent's stagger drives the entrance.
Anchor target #curriculum for the tab nav.

motion.notes: Desktop-only decorative tilted photo — uses fade-in (no upward translate) so the rotation doesn't fight the motion.

components.notes: Anchor target #eligibility for the tab nav. Decorative images stay hidden on mobile (lg:block).
Static checklist row. Animated via parent stagger only.
No tab anchor — instructors not in the tab list.

motion.notes: Wrap avatar img in a group parent; avatar uses group-hover:scale-105.

actions.rationale: Instructors are linkable trainer profiles. Slug derived from name. Mark unresolved — confirm trainer profile route exists.

components.notes: If a TrainerCard primitive exists from the /trainers harness, prefer reusing it and inherit its interaction model rather than redefining hover here.

Anchor target #faq. Reuse shadcn Accordion if present in apps/web/src/components/ui/accordion.tsx — it already provides keyboard-accessible expand/collapse. Otherwise, custom implementation with AnimatePresence + height auto.

motion.notes: When open state toggles, animate height 0 ↔ auto + opacity. Use AnimatePresence with initial={false}.
Rotate 180deg when open. Use data-[state=open]:rotate-180 if using Radix Accordion, otherwise drive via a local className toggle.

actions.rationale: Standard accordion expand/collapse. First item defaultOpen=true per decomposition.

components.notes: If shadcn Accordion is reused, its built-in Radix animation is acceptable — skip framer-motion accordion-expand and rely on Radix's data-state CSS transitions instead. Builder picks one path; don't double-animate.

motion.notes: Wrap card in `group`; image uses group-hover:scale-105 with overflow-hidden on the image container.

actions.rationale: Whole card clickable; href comes from course data (e.g. /courses/weight-loss-coach, /courses/mudra-therapy, /courses/mat-pilates). Mark unresolved — these routes may not exist yet.
Explicit CTA link mirrors the card click target. Use e.stopPropagation if both root and link handle click; or make only one of them the clickable target (prefer wrapping the card in a single <Link>).

components.notes: Prefer wrapping the entire card with a Next <Link>; the inner 'View Program' anchor becomes visual-only (hover-underline still applies).

actions.rationale: 'Try a class, free' → /try (matches decomposition prop). Confirm route exists.
From decomposition prop; confirm route exists.
From decomposition prop; confirm route exists.
From decomposition prop; confirm route exists.

motion.notes: Only applies if reused footer doesn't already style its links.

components.notes: Reuse existing site-footer if it exists. Hover-underline applied by the footer itself; this file just records the intent so the builder doesn't add a competing style.

smooth_anchor_scroll.where: Apply scroll-behavior: smooth on html (or via Tailwind 'scroll-smooth' class on the root <html>).
smooth_anchor_scroll.scroll_margin_top: Each anchored section (#overview, #highlights, #curriculum, #eligibility, #faq) sets scroll-mt-20 lg:scroll-mt-24 so the sticky tab bar doesn't cover the heading on jump.
smooth_anchor_scroll.reduced_motion: Wrap all framer-motion animations with the useReducedMotion hook OR set transition={{ duration: 0 }} when prefers-reduced-motion is true. Tailwind transitions auto-respect this via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) which is standard.

active_tab_observer.strategy: IntersectionObserver watches each anchored section. When a section crosses the rootMargin '-40% 0px -55% 0px' (i.e. roughly the middle of the viewport), set CourseTabs' activeTab state to its id.
active_tab_observer.fallback: If IO is unavailable (very old browsers), default to mount-time active tab and only respond to clicks.

unresolved.reason: Tab label 'Overall' has no obvious section to anchor to. Best guess: scroll to FAQ. Confirm with design — could equally be intended as a summary/closing-CTA anchor.

Figma's desktop tabs sit at y=900 with backdrop-blur but the file doesn't expose a 'sticky' prototype reaction. Defaulting to sticky lg:sticky lg:top-0; visual-QA should confirm scroll behavior matches design intent.
Card click navigates to /trainers/[slug] but trainer detail routes may not be implemented yet. Confirm route exists or downgrade to a no-op static card.
Hrefs /courses/weight-loss-coach, /courses/mudra-therapy, /courses/mat-pilates come from decomposition data — these route pages may not exist yet. Confirm or stub with a coming-soon landing.
Href '/try' from decomposition — confirm a /try route exists for the 'Try a class, free' CTA.
Hrefs /free-trial, /contact, /assessment from decomposition — confirm these routes exist.

Decomposition flags the green 'Reserve Your Spot Now' CTA may be hidden on desktop. If so, the mount fade-in-up still fires but on an invisible element — wrap in the same display-toggle as the visual layer so motion respects breakpoint.
If shadcn Accordion (Radix) is reused, its data-state CSS transitions are used; otherwise the framer-motion accordion-expand primitive runs. Builder must pick one path to avoid double-animation.
